purpose: End-to-end health check script that runs every manifest tool's detect command and four functional cross-tool checks (C compile+run, uv venv creation, tsx TypeScript execution, docker hello-world); exits non-zero on any core failure, making it a mandatory pre-push gate.
invariants: Uses set -uo pipefail but NOT set -e — all checks run to completion before tallying results
Exit code is 0 only when FAIL count is 0; WARN count does not affect exit code
manifest/tools.json must exist — the script aborts immediately if it cannot be found
Optional tools (status='optional' in manifest) produce WARN on detect failure, never FAIL
PATH is augmented at startup with ~/.local/bin, ~/tools/bin, and ~/.cargo/bin for full tool visibility
Self-locating: REPO_ROOT is resolved from the script's real path via readlink -f
gotchas: docker hello-world check is skipped (WARN) if the docker daemon is unreachable — this is expected after a fresh bootstrap before log-out/in for the docker group to take effect
detect commands are executed via 'timeout 60 bash -c' — commands that hang will be killed after 60 seconds and counted as failures
The temporary directory for functional checks is created with mktemp -d and cleaned up via EXIT trap — test artifacts never persist
Phase 1 reads the manifest via a jq process substitution; large manifests with slow detect commands can make this phase take over a minute

key_functions.name: Phase 1 (manifest tools)
key_functions.summary: Iterates every manifest entry, runs its detect command with a 60s timeout, and marks each as ok/warn/fail based on exit code and status field

key_functions.name: Phase 2 (functional checks)
key_functions.summary: Runs four cross-tool checks: gcc C compile/run, uv venv creation, tsx TypeScript one-liner, and docker hello-world container
